title: United CEO Scott Kirby Joins Congressional Caucus to Discuss Sustainable Aviation Business Case
content: Representatives Sharice Davids (D-KS) and Buddy Carter (R-GA), co-chairs of the bipartisan Congressional Sustainable Aviation Caucus, hosted a high-level briefing featuring United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby. The session moved beyond environmental theory to establish a firm business case for Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) and emerging aerospace technologies. Kirby emphasized that investments in SAF are not merely about carbon reduction but are vital for lowering long-term operational costs, securing American energy dominance, and linking rural agricultural health to the future of the aerospace industry. The discussion highlighted the intersection of sustainability, national security, and economic growth. Representative Davids noted that building a more sustainable system is essential for strengthening U.S. supply chains and keeping the nation at the forefront of global innovation. This policy forum also addressed the potential of advanced air mobility, including eVTOLs and hydrogen-powered flight, as key drivers for future job creation. By bringing together industry leaders and policymakers, the Caucus aims to create a resilient aviation network that can achieve net-zero goals by 2050 while maintaining global competitiveness. Ultimately, the briefing underscored that smart investments in clean technology provide the "certainty" needed for the private sector to scale production, benefiting American farmers, fuel producers, and the broader economy alike
author: Analysts Account
